sn10.8:1.1: I have heard that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Rājagaha in the Cool Forest.
sn10.8:1.2: Now at that time Anāthapiṇḍika the householder had arrived in Rājagaha on some business.
sn10.8:1.4: “An Awakened One, they say, has appeared in the world,”
sn10.8:1.5: and he wanted to go right then to see the Blessed One. Then the thought occurred to him,
sn10.8:1.6: “Today is not the proper time to go to see the Blessed One.
sn10.8:1.7: Tomorrow I will go to see the Blessed One at the proper time.” With his mindfulness immersed in the Awakened One he lay down to sleep.
sn10.8:1.8: Three times he got up during the night, thinking it was light.
sn10.8:1.9: Then he went to the gate to the charnel ground.
sn10.8:1.11: When Anāthapiṇḍika the householder had left the city, the light vanished and darkness appeared. Fear, terror, & horripilation arose, and because of that he wanted to turn back.
sn10.8:1.12: Then Sivaka the yakkha-spirit, invisible, proclaimed:
sn10.8:3.3: Going forward is better for you,
sn10.8:4.1: The darkness then vanished for Anāthapiṇḍika and the light appeared. The fear, terror, & horripilation he had felt subsided.
sn10.8:7.2: a third time, the light vanished and darkness appeared. Fear, terror, & horripilation arose, and because of that Anāthapiṇḍika wanted to turn back.
sn10.8:7.3: Then for a third time, Sivaka the yakkha-spirit, invisible, proclaimed:
sn10.8:9.3: Going forward is better for you,
sn10.8:10.1: The darkness then vanished for Anāthapiṇḍika and the light appeared. The fear, terror, & horripilation he had felt subsided.
sn10.8:10.2: So Anāthapiṇḍika went to the Cool Forest.
sn10.8:11.1: Now at that time, the Blessed One—having gotten up as the night was ending—was pacing back & forth in the open air.
sn10.8:11.2: He saw Anāthapiṇḍika the householder coming from afar.
sn10.8:11.3: On seeing him, he got down from his meditation path and sat on a seat made ready.
sn10.8:11.4: As he was sitting there he said to Anāthapiṇḍika,
sn10.8:11.6: Then Anāthapiṇḍika, (thinking,) “The Blessed One is calling me by my given name!” threw himself down right there at the Blessed One’s feet and said to him, “Lord, I hope the Blessed One has slept in ease.”
sn10.8:12.3: who doesn’t adhere to sensual pleasures,
